roi_reason: Every preventable founder escalation currently leaves no trace, so the same interventions repeat and the founder stays the bottleneck. Wiring the ledger makes escalations measurable and gives the queue a ranked kill-list. Mechanical edit work, cheap tier, bounded to three files plus one receipt.
context_summary: Founder adopted (2026-07-16, Cowork session) a Founder Exception Ledger compiled from an advisor spec: every escalation to the founder gets one logged row (what stopped, trigger, founder input, missing capability, recurrence, permanent owner, closure condition), priority = frequency x founder cost x preventability, paired guardrail metrics (founder exception rate vs unsafe autonomy/reversal rate), and a closure definition-of-done requiring receipt-verified artifacts. File is committed at ledger/founder-exception-ledger.md with status DRAFT / SUBMITTED / UNVERIFIED. Gap: nothing wires it into daily operation — no machine-appendable mirror, no agent instruction to log rows at escalation time, no read-surface registration. This packet does the wiring only; it does not claim the exception-budget system is live or enforced, and it changes no authority levels.
task: 1) Read ledger/founder-exception-ledger.md (DRAFT v1.0) in full. 2) Create ledger/founder-exception-ledger.csv as a machine-appendable mirror with a header row only, columns exactly: id,date,exception,trigger,founder_input,missing_capability,recurrence,permanent_owner,closure_condition,priority_fxcxp,status. 3) Append a short section to AGENTS.md titled 'Founder escalation logging rule': any agent that escalates a decision to the founder must first append one row to ledger/founder-exception-ledger.csv (and mirror it into the md table) — agent-authored, under 30 seconds, append-only, existing rows are never rewritten. 4) Read data/agent_read_surfaces_v1.json, then register ledger/founder-exception-ledger.md as a read surface following the file's existing entry shape; if the shape is ambiguous, do not guess — propose the entry in the PR description instead and record that choice in the receipt. 5) If data/agent_read_surfaces_v1.json was touched, run scripts/verify_agent_read_staleness_v1.sh and capture raw output. 6) Open exactly one draft PR using .github/pull_request_template.md (motor_id + task_cell + lane) containing only these changes; leave the PR unmerged. 7) Write exactly one receipt to receipts/p0pgr/ with changed_files, commands_run, raw validator output, pr_url, pass_fail computed from evidence, and next_pointer naming the candidate follow-up packet (weekly paired guardrail-metrics report). 8) Keep the ledger's DRAFT status header byte-identical — adoption or LOCK marking is reserved to the founder.
constraints: draft PR only — leave it unmerged, no direct push to the default branch
ledger DRAFT status header stays intact; adoption marking is founder-only
ledger files are append-only; never rewrite or delete existing rows
forbidden_actions: no marking the ledger ADOPTED or LOCKED
no edits outside AGENTS.md, ledger/, data/agent_read_surfaces_v1.json, receipts/p0pgr/
no PASS based on self-report or prose-as-proof
stop_rule: Stop after the draft PR is open and exactly one receipt is written to receipts/p0pgr/. Metrics reporting and enforcement are separate future packets.
